questions.question:
Given that f(q) = q/|q|, and q is a non-zero real number, the value of [f(f(q)) - f(-q)] is

A. 0
B. -1
C. 1
D. 2 or -2
questions.to_find: value of the expression f(f(q)) - f(-q)

understand.plain: The function f(q) = q/|q| is called the signum (sign) function. It returns +1 when q is positive and -1 when q is negative. The question asks us to evaluate a combined expression involving the composition f(f(q)) and f(-q). We need to figure out what each piece equals and then subtract.

keywords.explain: f(q) = q/|q| divides a number by its own absolute value, giving +1 for positive inputs and -1 for negative inputs
applying f to the output of f; since f(q) is always +1 or -1, we apply f to one of those values
applying the sign function to the negation of q; negating q flips the sign, so f(-q) = -f(q)

given.aim: find the value of f(f(q)) - f(-q) for all non-zero real q

terms.meaning: the sign function that outputs +1 when q > 0 and -1 when q < 0
terms.connects: the building block for all subsequent computations in this problem

terms.meaning: composition: substitute the output of f back into f; since f always returns ±1, and |±1| = 1, f(1) = 1 and f(-1) = -1, so f(f(q)) = f(q)
terms.connects: shows that the signum function is idempotent: applying it twice gives the same result as applying it once

terms.meaning: negating q before applying f; since |q| = |-q|, we get f(-q) = -q/|q| = -f(q)
terms.connects: shows that f is an odd function, so f(-q) = -f(q)

given.plan: Simplify f(f(q)) using idempotence, simplify f(-q) using the odd-function property, then subtract to get the final expression in terms of f(q)

steps.title: Evaluate f(f(q))
steps.apply: Since f(q) = +1 when q > 0 and f(q) = -1 when q < 0, the output of f is always a nonzero real with |f(q)| = 1. Applying f again: f(f(q)) = f(q)/|f(q)| = f(q)/1 = f(q). So f(f(q)) = f(q).
steps.note: The signum function is idempotent: applying it twice is the same as applying it once.

steps.title: Evaluate f(-q)
steps.apply: Since |-q| = |q| for all q, we have f(-q) = (-q)/|q| = -(q/|q|) = -f(q). The sign function is odd.
steps.note: This is the defining property of odd functions: f(-q) = -f(q).

steps.title: Compute the expression f(f(q)) - f(-q)
steps.apply: Substituting the results: f(f(q)) - f(-q) = f(q) - (-f(q)) = f(q) + f(q) = 2*f(q). When q > 0, f(q) = 1 so the expression = 2. When q < 0, f(q) = -1 so the expression = -2.
steps.note: The value depends on the sign of q: it is +2 for positive q and -2 for negative q.

solution.result: The value is 2 when q > 0 and -2 when q < 0, so the expression equals 2 or -2. Answer: D.

questions.formula_note: No standard registry formula needed; the signum function's idempotence and oddness are derived directly from the definition.

questions.question:
If |-2X + 9| = 3, then a possible value of -(X^2 - 6) would be:

A. 30
B. -30
C. -42
D. 42
questions.to_find: a possible value of the expression -(X^2 - 6) given the absolute value equation

understand.plain: An absolute value equation of the form |expression| = c with c > 0 always splits into two cases: the inside equals +c or the inside equals -c. Once we find the two values of X, we substitute each into the target expression to find the possible values.

keywords.explain: |A| = c (c > 0) means A = c or A = -c, giving exactly two solutions
the question asks which answer option can be produced by the expression for at least one valid value of X
keywords.example: if X = 3 gives result 10 and X = 6 gives result -30, then both 10 and -30 are possible values

given.aim: find a possible value of -(X^2 - 6) when |-2X + 9| = 3

terms.meaning: the absolute value of (-2X + 9) equals 3, so the two cases are -2X + 9 = 3 and -2X + 9 = -3
terms.example: similar to |2t - 1| = 5 giving t = 3 or t = -2
terms.connects: gives exactly two candidate values for X to test in the target expression
the other valid input; substituting into -(X^2 - 6) gives the answer

given.plan: Solve the absolute value equation for both cases to get X = 3 and X = 6, substitute each into -(X^2 - 6), and identify which result matches an answer option

steps.title: Split the absolute value equation into two cases
steps.note: Both values satisfy the original equation: |-6+9|=|3|=3 and |-12+9|=|-3|=3.

steps.title: Evaluate -(X^2 - 6) for X = 3
steps.apply: -(3^2 - 6) = -(9 - 6) = -(3) = -3. This value does not appear in the answer options.
steps.note: This case does not produce one of the listed options.

steps.title: Evaluate -(X^2 - 6) for X = 6
steps.note: The question asks for a possible value, meaning at least one of the two cases must match an option.

questions.formula_note: Standard absolute value split (|A| = c gives A = c or A = -c) is the only technique needed here.

questions.question:
If |4X + 7| >= 5, then the values of X (in interval notation) are:

A. (-inf, -3] only
B. (-inf, -3] U [-1/2, +inf)
C. [-3, -1/2]
D. [-1/2, +inf) only
questions.to_find: the set of all real X satisfying the absolute value inequality |4X + 7| >= 5

understand.plain: An absolute value inequality of the form |expression| >= c (with c > 0) means the expression is either at least c or at most -c. This gives a union of two separate intervals, not a single connected range. Think of it as the points that are at distance c or more from the center.

keywords.explain: |A| >= c means A >= c OR A <= -c, producing a union of two rays extending outward from the boundary points
keywords.example: |x| >= 2 means x >= 2 or x <= -2, i.e., (-inf,-2] U [2,+inf)

keywords.explain: when the two cases of the inequality are disjoint, the solution is the union (all points satisfying either case)
keywords.example: x <= -3 or x >= -1/2 is written (-inf,-3] U [-1/2,+inf)

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 480 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='480' height='90' rx='8' class='bg'/><line x1='30' y1='45' x2='450' y2='45' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='38' x2='30' y2='52' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='450' y1='38' x2='450' y2='52' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='150' y1='38' x2='150' y2='60' stroke='#2C2C2A' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='310' y1='38' x2='310' y2='60' stroke='#2C2C2A' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='45' x2='150' y2='45' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='5'/><circle cx='150' cy='45' r='6' class='ok'/><line x1='310' y1='45' x2='450' y2='45' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='5'/><circle cx='310' cy='45' r='6' class='ok'/><text x='145' y='75' text-anchor='middle' class='ink sub'>-3</text><text x='310' y='75' text-anchor='middle' class='ink sub'>-1/2</text><text x='90' y='32' text-anchor='middle' class='ok tag'>X <= -3</text><text x='380' y='32' text-anchor='middle' class='ok tag'>X >= -1/2</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Number line showing solution set: the two rays X <= -3 and X >= -1/2 highlighted in green, with the middle interval [-3, -1/2] excluded

terms.meaning: the expression 4X + 7 is at least 5 units away from zero on the number line
terms.connects: splits into two separate linear inequalities to solve independently

terms.meaning: the inner expression is large and positive
the inner expression is large and negative

given.plan: Apply the rule |A| >= c <=> A >= c or A <= -c, solve each linear inequality separately, then write the union of both solution sets

steps.title: Apply the absolute value inequality rule
steps.formula_raw: |A| >= c  <=>  A >= c  OR  A <= -c   (for c > 0)
steps.note: This is the outward inequality: solutions lie outside the interval where the expression is between -5 and 5.

steps.title: Solve Case 1: 4X + 7 >= 5
steps.apply: Subtract 7 from both sides: 4X >= -2. Divide by 4 (positive, no flip): X >= -1/2. Solution: [-1/2, +inf).
steps.note: Dividing by a positive number preserves the inequality direction.

steps.title: Solve Case 2: 4X + 7 <= -5
steps.apply: Subtract 7 from both sides: 4X <= -12. Divide by 4: X <= -3. Solution: (-inf, -3].
steps.note: The boundary points X = -3 and X = -1/2 satisfy |4(-3)+7| = |-5| = 5 >= 5, so they are included (closed brackets).

steps.title: Combine the two cases
steps.apply: The full solution is the union of both intervals: X in (-inf, -3] OR X in [-1/2, +inf), written as (-inf, -3] U [-1/2, +inf). The middle region (-3, -1/2) does NOT satisfy the inequality.
steps.note: Quick check for middle: X = -1 gives |4(-1)+7| = |-4+7| = |3| = 3 < 5, confirming the middle is excluded.

solution.result: The solution set is (-inf, -3] U [-1/2, +inf). Answer: B.

questions.formula_note: The absolute value inequality rule |A| >= c <=> A >= c or A <= -c (outward split) is the core technique; it is the complement of the inward split |A| <= c <=> -c <= A <= c.

questions.question:
Which of the following functions describe the graph shown in the figure below?

The graph shows four panels P, Q, R, S. Panel P shows a V-shaped absolute value graph shifted left. Panel Q shows an absolute value graph opening upward and shifted right. Panel R shows an absolute value graph with vertex below the x-axis. Panel S shows an absolute value graph shifted right with vertex on the x-axis.

A. y = |x| + 1, y = -|x| - 1
B. y = |x - 1| - 1, y = |x| - 1
C. y = |x + 1| - 1, y = |x| - 1
D. y = |x - 1| - 1, y = |x + 1| - 1
questions.to_find: which pair of functions matches the given graph with four panels P, Q, R, S

understand.plain: An absolute value function y = |x - h| + k has its vertex (the lowest point of the V-shape) at (h, k). The graph is symmetric about the vertical line x = h. Shifting right by h moves the vertex right; shifting down by |k| (when k < 0) moves the vertex below the x-axis. Matching the vertex location and direction to each panel identifies the correct function.

keywords.explain: the lowest point of the V-shape is at (h, k); h controls horizontal shift and k controls vertical shift
keywords.example: y = |x - 1| - 1 has vertex at (1, -1), touching the x-axis at two points

keywords.explain: replacing x by (x - h) shifts the graph h units to the right (h > 0) or |h| units to the left (h < 0)
keywords.example: y = |x - 1| is the same V-shape as y = |x| but shifted 1 unit right

keywords.explain: adding k outside the absolute value raises (k > 0) or lowers (k < 0) the entire graph
keywords.example: y = |x| - 1 shifts the standard V-shape down 1 unit, putting the vertex at (0, -1)

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 480 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='480' height='200' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='60' y='22' text-anchor='middle' class='ink lbl'>y=|x-1|-1</text><text x='180' y='22' text-anchor='middle' class='ink lbl'>y=|x|-1</text><g transform='translate(10,30)'><line x1='0' y1='80' x2='100' y2='80' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='50' y1='0' x2='50' y2='160' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='0,80 50,130 100,80' fill='none' stroke='#7F77DD' stroke-width='3'/><circle cx='60' cy='130' r='4' class='hi' stroke='#7F77DD'/><text x='60' y='145' text-anchor='middle' class='mut sub'>(1,-1)</text></g><g transform='translate(130,30)'><line x1='0' y1='80' x2='100' y2='80' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='50' y1='0' x2='50' y2='160' stroke='#B4B2A9' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='0,30 50,80 100,30' fill='none' stroke='#1D9E75' stroke-width='3'/><circle cx='50' cy='80' r='4' fill='#1D9E75' stroke='#1D9E75'/><text x='50' y='95' text-anchor='middle' class='mut sub'>(0,-1)</text></g><text x='60' y='182' text-anchor='middle' class='ok tag'>vertex (1,-1)</text><text x='180' y='182' text-anchor='middle' class='ok tag'>vertex (0,-1)</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Two side-by-side V-shaped graphs: the left graph y=|x-1|-1 with vertex at (1,-1) shifted one unit right and one unit down; the right graph y=|x|-1 with vertex at (0,-1) centered on the y-axis and one unit below the x-axis

given.aim: identify which pair of absolute value functions matches the given graph

terms.meaning: V-shaped graph with vertex at (1, -1): shifted 1 unit right and 1 unit down from the standard |x|
terms.example: zero-crossings at x = 0 and x = 2 (where |x-1| - 1 = 0 => |x-1| = 1 => x = 0 or x = 2)
terms.connects: matches the panel that shows a V with vertex below the x-axis and shifted to the right

terms.meaning: standard V-shape shifted 1 unit down, with vertex at (0, -1)
terms.connects: matches the panel that shows a symmetric V centered on the y-axis with its lowest point at y = -1

terms.meaning: general form where (h, k) is the vertex; h shifts horizontal, k shifts vertical
terms.example: y = |x + 1| - 1 has vertex at (-1, -1) — shifted LEFT, not right
terms.connects: used to distinguish between options B, C, D which differ only in the sign inside the absolute value

given.plan: Read off the vertex coordinates from each panel in the graph, then match to the function y = |x - h| + k where (h, k) is the vertex

steps.title: Recall the vertex form and how shifts work
steps.formula_raw: y = |x - h| + k  has vertex at (h, k)
steps.apply: A positive h inside shifts the vertex right; a negative k outside shifts it down. For option B: y = |x - 1| - 1 has vertex at (1, -1) and y = |x| - 1 has vertex at (0, -1).
steps.note: Note that |x - (-1)| = |x + 1| shifts LEFT to vertex at (-1, k), not right.

steps.title: Eliminate options A, C, D
steps.apply: Option A: y = |x| + 1 has vertex at (0, 1) — vertex is ABOVE the x-axis, inconsistent with graph showing vertex below. Option C: y = |x + 1| - 1 has vertex at (-1, -1) — shifted LEFT; graph shows rightward shift. Option D: both functions have vertices at (1,-1) and (-1,-1) — one is left-shifted; graph does not match.
steps.note: Reading vertex positions directly from graph panels is the fastest elimination strategy.

steps.title: Verify option B
steps.formula_raw: y = |x - 1| - 1: vertex (1, -1); zero-crossings x = 0, x = 2. y = |x| - 1: vertex (0, -1); zero-crossings x = 1, x = -1.
steps.apply: y = |x - 1| - 1 touches the x-axis at x = 0 and x = 2 with vertex at (1, -1). y = |x| - 1 is symmetric about the y-axis with vertex at (0, -1) and touches the x-axis at x = +/-1. Both match the shapes visible in the given graph panels.
steps.note: The graph panels P and Q or R and S correspond to these two functions in the correct positions.

solution.result: The two functions y = |x - 1| - 1 and y = |x| - 1 match the given graph. Answer: B.

questions.formula_note: The vertex form y = |x - h| + k is the key formula; no registry entry is needed beyond the standard absolute value definition.
